records.ety: [From Dead; cf. AS. d<?/dan to kill, put to death. See Dead, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deadened (dd"'nd); p. pr. & vb. n. Deadening.]

defs: To make as dead; to impair in vigor, force, activity, or sensation; to lessen the force or acuteness of; to blunt; as, to deaden the natural powers or feelings; to deaden a sound.
quotes: As harper lays his open palm Upon his harp, to deaden its vibrations.

defs: To lessen the velocity or momentum of; to retard; as, to deaden a ship's headway.
To make vapid or spiritless; as, to deaden wine.
To deprive of gloss or brilliancy; to obscure; as, to deaden gilding by a coat of size.
To render impervious to sound, as a wall or floor; to deafen.
